'''Refnar''' was a celestial body<ref name="CRO" /> located in the [[Refnar system/Legends|Refnar system]], a part of the [[Pakuuni sector/Legends|Pakuuni sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> During the early [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], it was the site of a [[Unidentified Alliance base (Refnar)|Rebel Alliance base]].<ref name="CRO" /> At some point by [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],<ref name="1 BBY">According to ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] did not yet utilize [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]] at the [[Time/Legends|time]] the "[[Last Stand at Refnar]]" took place. ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' establishes that the Alliance acquired the resources necessary to start the production of [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing fighters]] a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], which translates to [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Therefore, the engagement at Refnar must have occurred at some point by 1 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] discovered the facility and launched an [[Last Stand at Refnar|attack]] on it. Amid the ensuing confrontation between the [[Alliance Military/Legends|Alliance]] and [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial forces]] that became known as the "Last Stand at Refnar," most of the Rebel [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], [[Troop transport/Legends|troop transports]], and [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] were able to escape from the Refnar [[Star system/Legends|system]].<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Refnar was created by [[Ed Stark]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Refnar system, and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square T-6.<ref name="TEA" />
